summary:
Held 0 of today's 10 biggest UK/EU movers.

• 6 upside misses — RS1.L +15%, SHAW.L +7%, ASML.AS +6%, MKS.L +6%, STMPA.PA +6%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 4 downside moves (AEP.L -21%, CWR.L -9%, WKL.AS -5%, KNOS.L -4%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: AEP.L (-20.90%) — Indonesian Government unveiled CPO export-policy proposals on 20 May 2026; AEP.L's RNS acknowledged indirect pricing risk to domestic CPO sales, triggering a -21% sell-off on regulatory uncertainty around scope and implementation. Why we passed: Company RNS stated 'no direct impact', muting NLP sentiment below the news-reactive trigger threshold. Momentum-trader had no short signal — stock sat near 52-week highs with no prior negative drift; bot lacks short capability to act on a sudden gap-down.

top_movers.catalyst: FY26 annual results: adj. operating profit £265M beat consensus; cash conversion 109% vs 80% target; £100M buyback launched; Distrelec synergies £40M+ exceeded expectations.
top_movers.miss_reason: RS1.L was almost certainly inside an earnings-blackout gate — systematic strategies typically halt new positioning in names reporting full-year results to avoid binary event risk. The move was priced in at open; no clean intraday entry signal remained for momentum-trader (gap too large, RSI spiked) or news-reactive (pr
top_movers.suggestion: News-reactive earnings gate should auto-release names with a results-day open gap ≥ +5% that sustains price above the VWAP for ≥ 45 min post-open, allowing continuation entries on validated beats rather than blocking the name the entire session.

top_movers.catalyst: Delayed institutional re-rating of Shawbrook's 14 May Q1 update (loan book +2.6% to £19.7bn, FY26 ROTE ~17% reaffirmed, maiden dividend guided for FY27) combined with pre-AGM positioning ahead of the 21 May AGM.
top_movers.miss_reason: Momentum filters excluded SHAW.L after a ~40% drawdown from its 518p 52-week high; news-reactive's stale-signal window had already expired 6 days after the 14 May RNS; mean-reverter's minimum-volume or RSI-recovery threshold likely blocked entry as the stock drifted near its 52-week low.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive's catalyst re-evaluation to 7 sessions: re-arm entry if price stays within 2% of the results-day close on days N+1..+6 then breaks higher on ≥1.3× 30-day average volume.

top_movers.catalyst: UBS double-upgraded ASML to Buy and raised its price target from €1,600 to €1,900, naming it Europe's top semiconductor pick; shares gapped ~2.8% pre-market and extended to +5.91% by close.
top_movers.miss_reason: The move was priced in pre-market via a broker-note catalyst the news-reactive strategy likely has no feed for. Momentum-trader was probably blocked by an RSI overbought filter — ASML had already rallied ~98% in the prior 52 weeks — and a gap-up ceiling that rejected the open-print.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a tier-1 analyst-upgrade signal to news-reactive: trigger entry when a top-10 broker raises rating by ≥2 notches OR lifts PT by ≥15%, and extend the gap-up allowance parameter from its current ceiling to 3% to accommodate pre-market analyst-driven gaps.

top_movers.catalyst: FY2026 full-year results released 20 May beat consensus: adj. pre-tax profit £671.4M vs £665M est.; Q4 clothing LFL +4.3%, food +6.7%; dividend raised to 4.2p; FY2027 profit growth guided.
top_movers.miss_reason: Scheduled earnings day: most strategies gate MKS.L to avoid binary event risk. The gap materialised at the open (+5–6%) on pre-market RNS, breaching typical intraday gap-size filters; news-reactive requires post-open signal confirmation and cannot pre-position on pre-market prints.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an earnings-beat release rule to news-reactive: if pre-market RNS shows adj. profit > consensus by ≥1%, lift the earnings gate and allow entry on the first 5-min bar after open, capped at 0.5× normal position size to contain binary-event tail risk.

top_movers.catalyst: Pre-Nvidia-earnings semiconductor sector rally (NVDA reporting Q1 FY26 after close); ASML's same-day UBS upgrade to top European pick dragged correlated Paris-listed chip names including STMPA higher.
top_movers.miss_reason: Up ~67% in a month on back-to-back gaps (May 4 +6%, May 13 +9.4%); RSI likely hit momentum-trader's ceiling. News-reactive already fired on STM's Q1 beat and Starlink deal; today's move was a sector lift with no fresh STM-specific signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an NVDA-earnings event window to momentum-trader: on the day of Nvidia's after-close report, lift the RSI ceiling for European semiconductor proxies (STMPA.PA, IFX.DE) if sector ETF intraday volume exceeds 1.2× its 20-day average.

top_movers.catalyst: Post-earnings continuation drift: WOSG beat FY2026 consensus on May 14 (rev £1.83B, +13% CC, US +24%) with EBIT ahead of expectations; lagged analyst price-target upgrades are driving residual institutional buying six sessions later.
top_movers.miss_reason: The May 14 earnings gap (~15%) almost certainly triggered an earnings-gate lockout across all strategies. Today's drift has no discrete fresh catalyst, so news-reactive skips it; post-gap volume and RSI conditions fall outside momentum-trader's standard breakout filter.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-earnings re-entry window to momentum-trader: after a results-day gap ≥+10%, re-enable the name after 3 sessions if price holds above the gap close and volume normalises to ≥0.8× its 20-day average, to capture analyst-upgrade drift.

top_movers.catalyst: Indonesian Government unveiled CPO export-policy proposals on 20 May 2026; AEP.L's RNS acknowledged indirect pricing risk to domestic CPO sales, triggering a -21% sell-off on regulatory uncertainty around scope and implementation.
top_movers.miss_reason: Company RNS stated 'no direct impact', muting NLP sentiment below the news-reactive trigger threshold. Momentum-trader had no short signal — stock sat near 52-week highs with no prior negative drift; bot lacks short capability to act on a sudden gap-down.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a CPO-policy regulatory feed to news-reactive; when an Indonesian government commodity-export announcement is detected, override neutral RNS sentiment scores and flag plantation names for short-side review within 30 minutes of open.

top_movers.catalyst: Pre-AGM positioning pressure (shareholder meeting 2026-05-21, €2.52 dividend vote) compounding persistent AI-disruption sentiment; JPMorgan flagged UpToDate facing churn/pricing risk, driving WKL -33% YTD.
top_movers.miss_reason: WKL is in a structural downtrend with no intraday news catalyst — news-reactive requires a fresh signal, not trend continuation. Long-only constraint blocks momentum-trader from acting on negative momentum; mean-reverter's falling-knife filter suppresses longs on names trading >20% below 200-DMA.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a pre-AGM event gate: suppress mean-reverter long entries on any name with a major shareholder vote within 48 h, as event-window selling systematically biases these days negative and invalidates the reversion assumption.

top_movers.catalyst: FY2026 adj PBT margin fell 2.4pp to 16% (from 18%) on contractor/NI cost inflation and buyback suspension; post-results selling extended into T+2 as market weighted margin deterioration over the revenue/bookings beat.
top_movers.miss_reason: News-reactive saw May 18 results but the muted +0.43% T+0 reaction was below any short-signal threshold; control-rule-based post-earnings blackout (typically 2-3 days) was still active on T+2, locking KNOS.L out during today's margin-driven drift leg.
top_movers.suggestion: Shorten post-earnings blackout to 1 day for names where T+0 reaction is <±1%, then allow news-reactive a T+1/T+2 short entry if adj margin contracted >1pp AND buyback guidance was cut, with stop at the results-day intraday high.
